signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for May 27. Despite NZIER shadow board backing a hold at 2.25%, the market prices future hikes and a restrictive real rate deficit of -0.83%. If RBNZ delivers a hawkish hold or signals imminent tightening, short-squeeze risk is elevated as specs are crowded short (28% long). NZD/USD at 0.5874.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Options
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.85 supported by +278bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage. Trump headlines regarding a 'largely negotiated' Iran deal and Strait of Hormuz reopening have triggered a 5% tumble in WTI, easing safe-haven JPY demand. Options market remains cautious with 25d RR at -1.22 (moderate put skew) despite neutral VIX at 16.7. Carry dominates while 160.00 remains the intervention watch level.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Lagarde Headline + Volatility Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1641 as Lagarde flags inflation forecast revisions ahead of the June 11 meeting. IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.9% creates a +2.2% Vol Risk Premium, signaling the options market is pricing elevated protection relative to realized movement. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.00% (+175bp) carry advantage favors USD, structurally limiting upside from hawkish ECB rhetoric.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Data Shock
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7166 faces late-trend exhaustion as COT LF net long reaches +57,180 (74.5% long extreme). AUD Unemployment surprise (z=+2.60σ) contradicts the RBA's hiking stance (4.35%). Options market is heavily hedging downside with a +4.9% VRP (IV 13.8% vs HV30 8.9%) and -0.79 RR skew. High-conviction long positioning is vulnerable to a washout if data continues to miss.

signals.title: CHF/JPY — Retail vs Options Divergence
signals.text: CHF/JPY at 203.113 shows extreme retail sentiment with 88% short (contrarian long signal). However, the SNB 0.0% vs BoJ 0.75% (-75bp) carry deficit favors JPY. With VIX at 16.7 (normal volatility), the JPY carry bid competes with CHF safe-haven flows. Watch for retail capitulation if the pair holds above 203.00.
